description: A bounded symbolic example where the general residual R_general is not identically zero, but the model projection P_model(R_general) evaluates to zero. This demonstrates that MODEL_SPECIFIC_EQUIVALENCE may be authorized while GENERAL_SYMBOLIC_IDENTITY must be denied.

symbolic_example.model_projection: P_model: {z → x} i.e., set z = x

domain_note.motivation: Rice–Mele model sets specific algebraic relationships between tensor components that are not preserved in the general three-band case. The projection z→x is a domain label for one such relationship.
domain_note.clarification: This fixture uses a minimal symbolic example independent of any copyrighted material. The Rice-Mele domain label serves only as transparent motivation.

projection_comparison.reason: Projection from general three-band to two-band Rice-Mele is non-injective. Agreement under projection does not imply agreement in the general domain.

test_rules.model_specific_allowed: The claim may be promoted to MODEL_SPECIFIC_ONLY with projection context
test_rules.general_identity_denied: The claim must not be promoted to ACTIVE_VERIFIED with GENERAL_SYMBOLIC_IDENTITY scope
test_rules.injectivity_required: To promote a projection result to general identity, injectivity must be explicitly established
test_rules.separate_claim_needed: A GENERAL_SYMBOLIC_IDENTITY claim cannot be demoted to MODEL_SPECIFIC_ONLY; create a new projected claim instead
